title: Track current-cycle deferred beads and style deferred scheduler log lines
description: Extend `TuiSchedulerReporter` to maintain current-cycle deferred bead state from `bead_deferred(...)`, clear that state at cycle start, and emit deferred log entries with distinct styling so the log panel can visually distinguish deferrals from other events.
acceptance_criteria: `src/agent_takt/tui/app.py` maintains a `_deferred_this_cycle` set that is cleared at cycle start and populated by `bead_deferred(...)`.
Deferred log entries are emitted with a distinct style or markup intended for dim/grey rendering in the scheduler log panel.
The deferred-cycle state is exposed through existing TUI render flow so bead-row rendering can consume it in a later bead.

handoff_summary.completed: Added `_deferred_this_cycle: set[str]` to `TuiSchedulerReporter.__init__`. Modified `_post` to accept a `style` parameter and to clear `state.deferred_this_cycle` at the start of each cycle (when the cycle header is logged). Updated `bead_deferred` to populate `_deferred_this_cycle`, sync the copy into `state.deferred_this_cycle`, and emit the log line wrapped in `[dim]...[/dim]` Rich markup. Added `deferred_this_cycle: set[str]` (public) field to `TuiRuntimeState` in `state.py` so the bead-row render flow can consume it in a downstream bead.
handoff_summary.remaining: A downstream bead (per the spec) will consume `state.deferred_this_cycle` in bead-row rendering to visually mark deferred beads in the tree panel.
handoff_summary.risks: Thread safety of `state.deferred_this_cycle`: the reporter runs in a worker thread and assigns a new set object to the field. Under CPython's GIL, the pointer assignment is atomic, so reads from the main thread are safe. This would need a lock under a free-threaded interpreter.
handoff_summary.next_action: A downstream bead should read `state.deferred_this_cycle` from within `_populate_bead_tree` (or its label helpers) to apply visual styling to deferred bead rows in the tree panel.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: state.py was not in the original `expected_files` list, but the change is additive (new field only) with no risk to sibling beads modifying the same area.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Chose to store the deferred set both on the reporter (`_deferred_this_cycle`) and on `TuiRuntimeState.deferred_this_cycle`. The reporter's private copy is the source of truth during a cycle; the state copy is an atomic snapshot updated on each deferral so the render thread always sees a consistent set. Clearing `state.deferred_this_cycle` at cycle start (inside `_post`'s header-logging branch) means the first event of a cycle always resets stale data from the previous cycle. An alternative would have been to only store the set on the reporter and expose it via a method, but the state-field approach is consistent with how `scheduler_log` is shared between reporter and TUI. The `style` parameter on `_post` was added as a keyword-only argument to keep the public call sites unchanged while allowing styled variants.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) `state.deferred_this_cycle` is empty before any cycle runs; (2) after `bead_deferred` is called, the bead_id appears in `state.deferred_this_cycle`; (3) at the start of the next cycle (first `_post` call on a fresh reporter), `state.deferred_this_cycle` is cleared; (4) deferred log entries in `state.scheduler_log` are wrapped in `[dim]...[/dim]`; (5) non-deferred entries are not wrapped.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The `deferred_this_cycle` state persists between scheduler cycles until the next cycle's first `_post` call. If no further scheduler events fire after deferrals (e.g. the cycle ends with only deferrals), the state correctly reflects the last cycle. The bead-row rendering that consumes this field is deferred to a downstream bead.

conflict_risks: state.py was not in the original `expected_files` list, but the change is additive (new field only) with no risk to sibling beads modifying the same area.

execution_history.summary: Extended `TuiSchedulerReporter` and `TuiRuntimeState` to track current-cycle deferred beads with distinct styling in the scheduler log panel.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
